title: Supply chain: unpinned production dependency versions allow silent upgrades
description: All 6 production dependencies (ajv, ajv-formats, chalk, commander, fs-extra, inquirer) used caret (^) ranges in package.json, permitting npm to resolve newer minor/patch versions than those recorded in the lockfile. A compromised minor-version release of any dependency could execute arbitrary code on developer machines via postinstall hooks or payload injection.
impact: Arbitrary code execution on developer machines when `npx prvc` is invoked, credential harvesting via process.env, or filesystem modification.
mitigation: All 6 production dependencies pinned to exact versions in package.json (v2.2.0). .npmrc sets save-exact=true to prevent future drift. prepublishOnly runs npm audit --audit-level=high. CI uses npm ci to enforce the lockfile. Mitigated in v2.2.0.
